description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, natural appearance, featuring a mix of white, gray, and rusty orange hues. The surface is uneven, with jagged protrusions and deep crevices, giving it a weathered and organic look. The coloration suggests mineral deposits, possibly quartz or marble, with areas of oxidation or iron staining. Some faces of the stone are smoother, while others display more pronounced textural variation and roughness. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and mass make it a substantial obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A large, rugged stone with white, gray, and orange coloration, featuring jagged and weathered surfaces. Its dense, irregular form makes it a prominent natural barrier or decorative feature.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's jagged, tooth-like protrusions and a prominent mix of white and orange mineral deposits.
description_view_1: This side shows a smoother, rounded surface with a dominant white coloration and subtle orange and gray streaks.
description_view_2: This angle reveals a more compact, less textured face with a blend of orange, gray, and white, and fewer deep crevices.
description_view_3: This view displays a broad, flat surface with a mix of gray and white, and a visible patch of orange-brown staining.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling with patches of orange-brown staining and gray streaks, creating a natural, non-repetitive pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible mineral staining, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements such as soil, gravel, or other stones.
